manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: cedar, deacon, polyester, swan, gymnastics, france, birch, nitrogen, hat.
Your opponent's team words are: hockey, mercury, peru, wool, swallow, christmas, bishop, nuclear.
Distractor words are: ostrich, volleyball, korea, pine, pants, archbishop, acid.
The kill words are: coal.

Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

content.thinking:
I need to give a clue for "deacon" - this is my last word.

Deacon is a religious position in the Christian church hierarchy, below a priest. I need to avoid:
- opponent words: mercury, nuclear
- distractors: ostrich, korea, pine, pants, archbishop, acid
- kill word: coal

The problem is "archbishop" is a distractor - it's also a church position but higher in the hierarchy than deacon. I need to be careful not to lead my teammate to archbishop.

Some possible clues:
- "church" - relates to deacon, but might also relate to archbishop
- "clergy" - same issue
- "minister" - could work, deacon is a minister
- "ordained" - deacons are ordained
- "priest" - deacons can become priests, but this might be confusing
- "sermon" - deacons can give sermons
- "vestments" - deacons wear vestments
- "altar" - deacons serve at the altar
- "service" - deacons perform service

Actually, thinking about the hierarchy: archbishop > bishop > priest > deacon

The opponent has had "bishop" which was already guessed (wait, let me check - no, bishop is still in opponent's remaining words according to earlier rounds... wait, let me re-read)

Looking at the current state:
- Remaining opponent words: mercury, nuclear
- bishop is NOT in the remaining opponent words, so it must have been guessed already

So the main risk is "archbishop" which is a distractor.

I could use:
- "liturgy" - relates to deacon's role
- "servant" - deacons are servants in the church (deacon comes from Greek diakonos meaning servant)
- "ministry" - deacons perform ministry
- "pastoral" - relates to church work

Actually, "servant" might be good because:
- It relates to deacon (the word deacon literally means servant)
- It's unlikely to relate to mercury, nuclear, ostrich, korea, pine, pants, or acid


- Archbishop is a high-ranking position, so "servant" wouldn't naturally point there

I think "servant" is my best option - it captures the core meaning of deacon without accidentally leading toward archbishop or any of the other distractors.
